title: hesperis pygmaea
intro: Hesperis pygmaea, also known as Dwarf Evening Primrose, is a low-growing perennial plant in the Brassicaceae family, characterized by its delicate white or pink flowers and sweet fragrance. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of Hesperis pygmaea include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and supporting the immune system. The plant can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and topical creams, and is often used in aromatherapy to promote relaxation and reduce stress.
